one_line_ask: Pre-flight four supplied traveller-themed t-shirt vector graphics for screen-print separation readiness -- verify transparent backgrounds, count distinct spot colors per design, check for registration marks and thin-line survivability, vectorize/inspect to flag raster contamination -- and emit a marked-up per-design seps audit report with a go/no-go recommendation for the exposure room.
brief: Wandergrain Supply Co., a small-batch apparel screen-print shop, has just received four traveller-themed t-shirt graphics from a freelance designer for its new "Modern Wanderer" tee line (universal travel icons -- a passport-stamp badge, a latitude/longitude coordinate mark, a minimalist skyline, and a compass-rose crest). Before any of these hit the exposure room and burn screens, the shop needs an independent pre-flight / separation audit -- the designer swears the files are "pre-flighted for screen print," but the shop has been burned before by art that looked fine on screen and fell apart on the press. We are NOT redesigning anything and NOT producing final films; we need an honest QA report. For each of the four designs, please verify and document: (1) background integrity -- the art must sit on a genuinely transparent background with no stray off-white halo or leftover matte that would print as a phantom screen; (2) spot-color / ink count -- count the distinct flat spot colors in each design and flag any design that exceeds our 4-color manual-press limit, or that hides near-duplicate colors that should be merged to one screen; (3) registration & trap readiness -- check whethe
